item.title: Shear viscosity of liquid Pb–Bi: a near-degenerate alloy test of the static Rice–Allnatt description
item.description: The Bretonnet–Silbert (BS) pseudopotential, with linearised Weeks–Chandler–Andersen (LWCA) perturbation theory and Rice–Allnatt (RA) transport theory, is a static, pairwise route to the magnitude of the shear viscosity of liquid sp metals. We use the ...

item.description: Polymer-derived ceramics (PDCs) offer a molecular-to-microstructure design pathway unavailable through powder processing, yet the quantitative relationships between preceramic polymer architecture and ceramic properties remain scattered across four ...

item.title: Revisiting the Vibrational Force Field of Matrix-Isolated
item.description: The 17-e anion, [Fe(CO)4]-, was first characterised in 1981 by Breeze et al1. We subsequently challenged their interpretation2, suggesting that they had produced [Fe(CO)3]-. In the light of new calculations here, we withdraw that view. We confirm that ...

item.title: BraggCalculator
item.description: Powder diffraction maps an atomic structure to an experimentally familiar fingerprint. Simulating that fingerprint is therefore a routine step in phase identification, structure screening, refinement, and machinelearning workflows. BraggCalculator is a ...

item.title: Rhamnolipid Biosurfactant for All-Green-Solvent-Processed Perovskite Solar Modules
item.description: Physical properties and toxicity of solvents remain barriers to scalable manufacturing of perovskite solar modules. In blade coating of thin films, solution viscosity and surface tension govern meniscus stability and wet-film quality. However, ...

item.description: Porous materials such as inorganic porous materials, hybrid nanoparticles, metalorganic frameworks, porous organic polymers, metal organic polyhedral, and porous organic cages have attracted significant attention due to their diverse applications in gas ...

item.title: Total Synthesis of Piperhancin B and Isofutoquinol
item.description: We report the first total synthesis of piperhancin B, a natural neolignan exhibiting promising nitric oxide production inhibitory activity. Remarkably, this natural product possesses a highly strained housane structure, which constitutes a huge synthetic ...

item.title: Continuous UV-Spectroscopic Interrogation of Enzymes Processing Phosphorylated Intermediates Through Modular Reaction Networks
item.description: Phosphorylated intermediates are ubiquitous across primary and secondary metabolism, yet continuous and versatile assay systems for the interrogation of the enzymes that use them are underdeveloped. To address this gap, we developed a system forPhosphate ...

item.title: Photochemical Induction of Trion-based Polaritons in Quasi-1D Inorganic Nanotubes
item.description: Strong coupling between optical cavity modes and excitons in transition-metal dichalcogenides (TMDs) has attracted considerable interest due to its potential applications in nanophotonics and nonlinear optical technologies. Owing to their high refractive ...

item.title: Which state are you converging? A spin audit of sample-based quantum diagonalization benchmarks on iron–sulfur clusters
item.description: Sample-based quantum diagonalization (SQD/QSCI) selects determinant subspaces from quantum-circuit samples for classical diagonalization; its iron–sulfur demonstrations anchor utility-scale quantum-chemistry claims, and a recent critique disputes them at ...

item.title: Fluorescent Dyes in Image-Guided Surgery: Chemical Properties, Targeting, and Biomedical Applications
item.description: Fluorescence-guided surgery (FGS) has emerged as a transformative intraoperative modality that enables real-time, optical visualization of target tissues to improve surgical precision and oncological outcomes. This review examines the chemical properties, ...

item.title: Coumarin acrylamide as a fluorescence switch-on monomer to assay kinetics of radical polymerizations and for bioimaging
item.description: Radical polymerizations are among the most widely used polymer synthesis methods and have become versatile reactions for modifying living cells with bioorthogonal macromolecules and creating artificial cells. However, to follow and monitor these reactions,...

item.title: Mechanochemical synthesis of C-1 deuterated aldehydes and α-deutero acyloins via NHC catalysis
item.description: α-Deutero acyloins and C-1 deuterated aromatic aldehydes are synthesized via NHC catalysed umpolung using D2O under mechanochemical conditions. The competing aldehyde deuteration and deutero-benzoin condensation are controlled by the steric hindrance of ...

item.title: Measuring the Membrane Protein-Bound Lipidome
item.description: Lipids play diverse physiological roles in energy storage, signaling, and cell structure. Another less appreciated role is as allosteric modulators of membrane proteins. However, it is challenging to determine the identify and affinity of lipids ...

item.title: A Stereochemistry-Aware Circular Algorithm and Fingerprint for Resonance-Invariant Molecular Representation
item.description: Algorithmic molecular fingerprints remain attractive for high-throughput chemoinformatics because they are fast, discrete, and easy to integrate into screening, similarity search, and machine-learning workflows. However, widely used circular fingerprints ...

item.title: On the Role of Hydroxide Ions as the Mediators of Cathodic Cu Corrosion
item.description: Understanding changes and degradation of the copper cathode during the electrochemical carbon dioxide reduction reaction (CO2RR) is a fundamental challenge. Advances in operando experimental methods and computational tools have shed light on the ...

item.title: Trapping Spontaneously Formed Knots on Heterogeneously Charged Linear Polymers
item.description: Metastable knots in linear polymers are difficult to achieve because topological entanglements rapidly relax and untie. Using simulations of triblock copolyelectrolytes, we show that knots formed during poor-solvent collapse can be trapped by quenching to ...

item.title: Nonequilibrium Electronic Response Modes Govern Catalytic Energetics under Illumination
item.description: While nonequilibrium electronic configurations generated under illumination are widely recognized as central to photocatalysis, how they govern catalytic reaction energetics remains poorly understood. Here, we develop a delta self-consistent field (ΔSCF)-...

item.description: Automated exploration of organometallic reaction mechanisms remains limited by the absence of molecular representations and enumeration rules that capture dative bonding, and by the computational cost of transition state refinement with large basis sets. ...

item.title: Raman Spectroscopic Characterization of the Benzene:Acetonitrile (3:1) Cocrystal Relevant to Titan, Saturn’s Moon
item.description: Titan, Saturn’s largest moon, hosts active organic chemistry and surface environments where small molecules are expected to crystallize as molecular minerals. Benzene and acetonitrile, both predicted to be present on Titan, were shown to form a ...

item.description: We develop an analytical theoretical framework for a triple-helix closed-loop hydrogen-bond network to elucidate the coupling between geometric topological constraints and the statistical and dynamical behaviors of the system. The triple-helical closed-...

item.description: Organic charge transfer cocrystals (OCTCs) are an appealing platform for solution processable electronics with tunable properties. Thanks to their modular, molecular composition, OCTC electronics and optics can be varied by the design and selection of ...

item.title: Cheméo Relay, a D-MPNN-based chemical property prediction model family
item.description: A message-passing neural network is a learned group-contribution method only if its readout carries molecular size, which the framework-default mean readout discards. We train directed message passing neural networks (D-MPNN, Chemprop v2) on experimental ...

item.title: Sulfonated nanoscale graphite as a catalyst for the alcoholysis of cellulose to ethyl levulinate
item.description: This study investigates the critical role of carbon catalyst surface architecture in the direct ("onepot") ethanolysis of microcrystalline cellulose to ethyl levulinate. Three sulfonated carbon matrices were synthesized and evaluated: pristine graphite, ...

item.title: Lamination Pressure Governs the Effectiveness of Sn Buffer Layers in Low-Pressure All-Solid-State Lithium Metal Batteries
item.description: All-solid-state lithium-metal batteries require stable Li plating/stripping under low stack pressures relevant to practical operation. Here, Sn was used as a model Li-alloying buffer layer to clarify how lamination pressure affects interfacial stability. ...

item.title: Deconstructing the distributional impacts of air pollution controls
item.description: Air quality policy increasingly seeks to reduce both population exposure and exposure disparities. For emissions-control policy, this requires identifying which sources, sectors, and locations have the greatest benefit per unit emissions. Here we develop ...

item.description: Radical polymers are an emerging class of organic electronic materials that offer electronic properties, making them attractive for transparent, biosensing, and energy storage applications. Charge transport in these materials occurs through redox-active ...

item.description: Chemical pollution has emerged as a major threat to freshwater sustainability; however, its impacts and associated responsibilities across global supply chains remain insufficiently understood. This study developed an assessment framework to trace water ...

item.description: Regarding the essential issue of the oscillator in Planck's blackbody thermal radiation, the free electron model (FEM) in potential box has been proposed. The results of nonlinear curve fitting have shown that FEM can describe the intensity distribution ...

item.description: Commercial quantum chemistry packages operate as opaque black boxes for most students. We present physcode, an open-source Python pedagogical interface built around PySCF that reads standard Gaussian-format .gjf input and writes Gaussian-compatible .log ...

item.description: Regioselectivity models are of key importance to the implementation of C–H functionalisations in synthetic route planning. In structure- and fragment-based drug discovery (SBDD/FBDD), ligand-target affinity is enhanced by elaborating weak hits into potent ...

item.description: Alan O’Riordan Precision Electrochemistry Nanosensor Group Tyndall National Institute, University College Cork Cork, Ireland 0000-0002-7554-1536 While generator-collector (GC) electrochemistry using traditional electrochemistry methods, such as cyclic ...

item.title: Absolute, fast, accurate and easy-to-use method for determining the injected samples volumes in capillary electrophoresis and related methods using an injection tracer
item.description: In this study, a simple, rapid, and reliable methodology was developed for measuring the injected sample volume in capillary electrophoresis and related techniques. The method does not require a calibration curve, nor knowledge of sample viscosity, ...

item.title: Concise and Modular Access to a Chiral Biaryl Monophosphine Library via Ir-Catalysed Atroposelective C–H Arylation and Downstream Use in Catalytic Enantioselective Transformations
item.description: Atropoisomeric biaryl monophosphines, chiral variant of Buchwald-type ligands are empowering ligands for asymmetric catalysis. However, their synthesis relies heavily on resolution or step-intense synthetic approaches restricting structural diversity and ...

item.title: Well-Defined Magnesium Complexes Enabled Hydroborative Cleavage of Monomeric and Polymeric Substrates
item.description: In this work, we synthesized β-diketiminate (NacNac)-supported magnesium complex (DippNacNaciPrMg.THF) (DippNacNac = (2,6-iPr2C6H3)NC(Me)C-(H)C(Me)N(2,6-iPr2C6H3)) (Mg-1) and amidinate-based magnesium complex PhC(NtBu)2MgiPr.THF (Mg-2) and investigated ...

item.title: Intranasal Delivery of Molecularly Engineered Nanoparticles for Temporal and Multi-Target Alzheimer's Therapy
item.description: Alzheimer’s disease (AD) is driven by a self-reinforcing cycle of amyloid-beta (Aβ) aggregation, cerebral hypoxia, and chronic neuroinflammation, which severely limits the efficacy of single-target interventions. Here, we develop a molecularly engineered ...

item.title: Synthesis of oxime, lactone, and lactam derivatives from 29-hydroxyfriedelan-3-one and their cytotoxic activity against breast cancer and leukemia cells
item.description: Cancer is the second leading cause of death worldwide, with breast cancer and leukemia among the most prevalent types. Some chemotherapy options are becoming obsolete due to multidrug-resistant cells and new tumor initiation mechanisms. We aimed to ...

item.title: Computational prediction of Y-doped Cd2Sb2O7 as a competitive Sb-based n-type Transparent Conducting Oxide
item.description: Transparent conducting oxides (TCOs) with diverse band alignments are essential for next-generation optoelec- tronic devices, where precise energy level matching and carrier type control are critical for efficient performance. In this work, we ...

item.title: Computationally Guided Donor-Engineering of Azahelicenes Towards High-Performance Circularly Polarized Luminescence
item.description: Developing circularly polarized luminescent (CPL) organic emitters that simultaneously exhibit high emission efficiency and strong chiroptical activity remain a significant challenge. Herein, we report a computationally guided design strategy for donor-...

item.title: Wearable electrofluidic biosensing system for real-time quantitation and adaptive stratification of fluctuating trace sweat antibodies
item.description: However, sweat antibody detection remains particularly challenging because antibody concentrations are substantially lower than those of common sweat markers such as cortisol and glucose. By integrating ultra-sensitive interfacial capacitive sensing with ...

item.title: Model Population Analysis to Optimize Near-Infrared-Based Screening of Deoxynivalenol in Wheat
item.description: The widespread contamination of wheat with deoxynivalenol (DON) necessitates rapid, non-destructive screening methods. Although near-infrared (NIR) spectroscopy offers a promising cost-effective solution, indirect detection via Fusarium-induced sample ...

item.title: Unraveling the Role of Macrocyclic Backbone in Cu–N–C Molecular Catalysts for Electrocatalytic CO2-to-CH4 Reduction
item.description: Cu–N–C molecular catalysts with well-defined Cu–N4 sites show strong potential for CO2-to-CH4 production, yet the role of the macrocyclic backbone remains unclear. Herein, we compare two representative Cu–N–C molecular catalysts, copper phthalocyanine (...

item.title: The Brewing Process and Hop Chemistry
item.description: Beer production is often represented as a sequence of unit operations, whereas product quality emerges from a coupled network of extraction, enzymatic conversion, thermal reaction, phase separation, microbial metabolism, and interfacial phenomena. Hops ...

item.title: Navigating Compositional Space in Palladium Hydride: Benchmarking Machine Learning Interatomic Potentials
item.description: Palladium plays a crucial role in advancing the hydrogen economy, serving in applications such as hydrogen purification, sensing, catalysis, and conversion. While machine learning approaches have shown great promise to accelerate first-principles ...

item.title: Phytochemical analysis and screening of biological activities of Anthemis lorestanica Iranshahr
item.description: This is the first report of studying phytochemicals and biological activity of the essential oil and different extracts of Anthemis lorestanica Iranshahr. Flower essential oil was analyzed using GC-MS. Total phenolic, flavonoid and tannin contents were ...

item.title: Elucidating the Dynamic Behavior of Surfactants: A Chemical Language Model Perspective
item.description: The practical utility of a surfactant is determined not only by its equilibrium behavior but also by how quickly it takes effect. Dynamic surface tension (DST)—the time evolution of the surface tension driven by surfactant adsorption onto a freshly formed ...

item.title: LIFE: Line-Shape Integrity and Fidelity Enhancement for Reconstructing Non-Uniformly Sampled Protein NMR Spectra
item.description: Non-uniform sampling (NUS) accelerates multidimensional NMR spectroscopy, but strong undersampling can introduce missing peaks, artifacts, and line-shape distortion. Here, we present LIFE, a self-supervised deep-learning framework for reconstructing two-...

item.title: Poxvirus F13L homodimer-interface targeted by BoltzGen cyclic-minipeptides and permeable mutants
item.description: Hundreds of Boltzgen cyclic-minipeptide-conformer candidates of different sizes targeted the vaccinia poxvirus F13L homodimer-interface (F13Lmin) in a Tecovirimat-binding manner. However, only low affinity candidates < 6-mer predicted membrane-...

item.title: From Hydrothermal Vents to Ocean Worlds: the Prebiotic Chemistry of Ketene
item.description: Living systems are sustained far from equilibrium by continuous, enzyme-mediated turnover of chemical fuels. How comparable chemistry could begin without evolved catalysts, and against the thermodynamic cost of assembling complex molecules in water, ...

item.title: Data-Driven Multi-Objective Analysis of Activity–Poisoning resistance Trade-Offs in Complex Multimetallic Electrocatalysts for Glucose Oxidation
item.description: The discovery of efficient and durable multimetallic nanoparticle electrocatalysts requires effective navigation of vast compositional spaces. However, systematically exploring these spaces while balancing competing performance objectives remains ...

item.title: Association-gated multiphoton activation of inert haloarenes via sequential electron donor–acceptor assembly
item.description: The functionalization of recalcitrant chemical bonds that require extreme reduction potentials is often thwarted by the absence of a defined reaction field to control potent intermediates. Conventional multiphoton photocatalysis generates super-reductants,...

item.title: Reconfigurable Peptide Nanostructures via Sequence and Solvent Tuning
item.description: Peptide self-assembly in aqueous media is dominated by hydrophobic collapse and backbone hydrogen bonding, further stabilized by side-chain-specific interactions such as cation-π, aromatic stacking, and electrostatics. However, under non-aqueous and low-...

item.title: BeamStop: A software for analysing chemical imaging and tomography data
item.description: Chemical imaging datasets, particularly those from techniques like X-ray powder diffraction imaging and tomography (XRD-CT), are challenging to analyse due to their high dimensionality, complexity, and spatial heterogeneity. BeamStop is a specialised ...

item.title: Dynamic solid-liquid equilibrium controls propylene electrochemical epoxidation on silver-based catalysts
item.description: Although silver-based catalysts are the benchmark for thermocatalytic epoxidation of propylene, they are considered as subpar compared to Pd-based catalysts for the electrocatalytic epoxidation of propylene. Recent reports have challenged this view, ...

item.title: Iterative Constructing and Screening of DNA-Encoded Libraries Enable the Discovery and Structure Optimization of Macrocyclic Hit Compounds
item.description: Macrocyclic peptides occupy a privileged "middle space" in drug discovery, offering the specificity of biologics combined with the synthetic tractability of small molecules. However, the rapid identification and structural optimization of macrocyclic ...

item.title: Soft Nano/Microgels Observed in situ at Fluid Interfaces:Recent Progress and Future Perspectives
item.description: In this invited focus review, recent progress in understanding the interfacial behaviors of soft nano/micro hydrogels (hereafter referred to as microgels) is reviewed, with particular emphasis on research done by the authors’ group. Unlike rigid polymeric ...

item.title: Late-Stage C4-Selective Pyridine Alkylation Enabled by Benzhydryl Activation

item.title: Features of the Gibbs Equation (∆G = ∆H - T∆S) Applied to Solvation, Vaporization, and Complexation Thermodynamics
item.description: The Gibbs equation (∆G= ∆H–T∆S) remains a cornerstone of chemical thermodynamics. Yet the scope and origins of the widely observed correlations between the enthalpies (∆H) and entropies (∆S), or Gibbs energies (∆G) and enthalpies, have long been ...

item.title: High-speed photoresponse of CVD-grown lateral TMD heterostructures
item.description: Although chemical vapor deposition (CVD) enables wafer-scale fabrication of transition metal dichalcogenide (TMD) photodetectors, CVD-grown devices are often limited by high defect densities and are generally considered significantly slower than devices ...

item.title: pH-Dependent Free Energies of Insulin Fibril Assembly from Protonation Ensemble Statistics
item.description: Protonation equilibria govern the pH-dependent stability of protein aggregates, yet their thermodynamic implications remain difficult to quantify. Here, we establish a direct connection between site-unresolved protonation statistics and pH-dependent free ...

item.title: Improved Aromatic Finkelstein Reactions for the Synthesis of Iodinated BODIPYs
item.description: Iodinated 4,4-difluoro-4-bora-3a,4a-diaza-s-indacenes (BODIPYs) are valuable synthetic intermediates for the formation of functionalised fluorophores, as they enable downstream nucleophilic aromatic substitution (SNAr) and metal-catalysed cross-coupling ...

item.title: Investigating the Stacking Diversity in 2D COFs: Implications for Structural and Elastic Properties
item.description: 2D covalent organic frameworks (COFs) are layered organic materials that often adopt multiple stacking configurations or exhibit disordered stacking motifs. The stacking type is known to have a significant influence on their properties. However, only a ...

item.title: Persistent Homology using Nets: Topologically Informed Machine Learning Descriptors of Framework Materials
item.description: Topological nets define the connectivity in framework materials, influencing their pore structure and mechanical properties. In this work, we investigate the importance of incorporating this information into machine learning models for gas adsorption ...

item.title: Operating Conditions Redefine MOF Design: Interpretable Windows Expose Cross-Process Conflict and Pressure-Driven Rank Reversal
item.description: High-throughput screening identifies which metal–organic framework (MOF) ranks highest under one set of conditions, but not whether its design logic survives another operating regime. Here, we discover compact descriptor windows across 278,407 ARC-MOF ...

item.title: Single-GPU ML/MM Metadynamics Captures Enzyme-Bound Carbohydrate Puckering
item.description: Machine-learning/MM simulations promise QM/MM-like conformational sampling at molecular-dynamics cost, but practical proof-of-concept examples for enzyme-bound carbohydrates remain scarce. Here, we evaluate a MACE/AMBER well-tempered metadynamics protocol ...

item.title: Lewis Acid-Mediated Defluorination of Fluorinated Diluents in Battery Electrolytes
item.description: Fluorinated diluents have been widely adopted in lithium metal batteries in recent years and represent one of the most successful strategies for stabilizing the electrode-electrolyte interphase. Because these molecules interact only weakly with alkali ...

item.title: Dynamics of liquid-liquid phase-separated microdroplets
item.description: Synthetic mimics of biological condensates serve as a versatile platform for understanding the dynamics of liquid-liquid phase separation (LLPS) and the functional biomolecules encapsulated within the condensed microenvironment. In recent years, owing to ...

item.title: A Hidden Deactivation Pathway of the Long-Lived MLCT State in Fe(HMTI)(CN)2 Revealed by Multireference Calculations
item.description: Iron complexes are attractive earth-abundant alternatives to noble-metal photosensitizers, yet their metal-to-ligand charge-transfer (MLCT) lifetimes are limited by fast nonradiative decay through low-lying metal-centered (MC) states. Fe(HMTI)(CN)2...

item.title: Single-and Multi-bead Coarse-grained Form Factors for SAXS Calculations
item.description: Small-angle X-ray scattering (SAXS) is widely used to study proteins in solution, but atomistic scattering calculations are computationally demanding for large systems and many-body simulations. We develop two coarse-grained, polynomial form factor models ...

item.title: Pose-coupled native-interaction weighting in kinase ensemble docking: EGFR enrichment and CDK2 transfer
item.description: Background. Ensemble docking can assign a high neural score to a ligand without showing whether the selected receptor-specific pose recovers interactions observed in target holo structures. We tested whether explicit recovery of target-native interactions ...

item.title: Two-Dimensional Materials beyond Graphene: Trends and Applications
item.description: Two-dimensional (2D) materials beyond graphene have emerged as a diverse and rapidly expanding family of atomically thin structures with unique properties derived from quantum confinement, reduced dielectric screening, and high surface-to-volume ratios. ...

item.title: G-Quadruplex-Inspired Suprazymes within Coacervates as Catalytic Protocells
item.description: Compartmentalized catalytic networks allow living systems to spatially organize chemical reactions, promote effective substrate concentration, intermediate transfer, and functional control. Constructing synthetic cells from abiotic building blocks that ...

item.title: Rearrangement of 2-(γ-aminopropyl)benzoxazoles into 2-(α-pyrrolinylamino)phenols: from serendipitous reaction discovery to anti-mycobacterial opportunities

item.title: TRACKING THE ULTRAFAST DYNAMICS OF CARBON-CARBON BOND FORMATION FOLLOWING CH3I PHOTODISSOCIATION ON AMORPHOUSSILICON OXIDE SURFACES
item.description: Carbon–carbon (C–C) bond formation at solid interfaces is central to heterogeneous catalysis and surface photochemistry, yet the ultrafast dynamics of radical coupling at surfaces remain poorly resolved. Here, we investigate the time-resolved formation of ...

item.title: Directed Evolution of Nonheme Fe Enzymes for Enantioselective and Regiodivergent 1,3- and 1,4-Nitrogen Migration: Biocatalytic Asymmetric Synthesis of Non-Canonical α- and β-Amino Acids
item.description: Biocatalyst-controlled regioselective C(sp3)–H functionalization of carboxylic acid derivatives provides a powerful method for the synthesis of non-canonical α- and β-amino acids. Herein, we report a nonheme Fe enzyme-catalyzed, regiodivergent and ...

item.title: Transfer learning for predicting protein binding affinities of metal-organic inhibitors
item.description: Metal-organic compounds have unique structural and electronic properties that make them attractive candidates for protein inhibition. However, the rational design and computational prioritization of such compounds remain challenging due to the limited ...

item.title: Assembly-by-Elimination of 3D Nanoarchitectures with Designed Interstitials and Interweaves
item.description: The realization of metamaterials with tailored functional responses requires three-dimensional (3D) architectures that combine low packing fractions with complex topology. Such metastructures can enhance strength-to-weight ratios, accommodate large ...
